data.title: Radiofrequency Ablation for Atrial Fibrillation in Advanced Chronic Heart Failure
data.brief_summary: Heart failure is a condition that occurs when the heart muscle weakens and no longer contracts normally. Half of these patients have an irregularity of heart rhythm called atrial fibrillation (AF). Patients with both heart failure and AF spend more time in hospital, and die earlier than those with heart failure alone. AF is difficult to treat with conventional methods in patients with heart failure. Radiofrequency ablation is a new technique used to cure AF. The investigators aim to establish if
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Informed consent
* Persistent atrial fibrillation (AF)
* New York Heart Association (NYHA) II, III and IV chronic heart failure (CHF) despite optimal medical therapy for at least 3 months
* left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) \<35% - as measured by radionuclide ventricul
endpoints: Change in Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction by Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)%
Baseline Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction by Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction by Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)at 6 Months
